standards_passed: no-hardcoded-values (Standard 2): THRESHOLD=75 and RATE_LIMIT=3 in userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh are pre-existing constants, not newly introduced; no new hardcoded paths in pretool-gitignore-preflight.py or test file

use-source-venv (Standard 3): python3 -c usage in userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh is pre-existing (confirmed by git diff vs origin/master); Standard 3 detection scope is .sh and .md files only — settings.json JSON config is out of scope; test_arch6_arch3.py subprocess.run([python3,...]) is in a .py test file outside Standard 3 scan scope
meaningful-naming (Standard 5): all four file names are descriptive and specific; no forbidden patterns (enhance-, fast-, optimize-, -v2, etc.) found

english-only (Standard 6, unconditional scan): agents/ and commands/ directories contain no Chinese characters; settings.json:22 Chinese comment (_comment_anthropic: 以下为可选配置（已禁用）) is pre-existing in origin/master and not newly introduced by this diff
merge-similar-scripts (Standard 7): pretool-gitignore-preflight.py has a unique gitignore-blocking purpose not duplicated by any existing hook
parameterized-scripts (Standard 9): no new unparameterized values introduced
dangling-references (Standard 10): hooks/pretool-gitignore-preflight.py and hooks/userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh referenced in the test file both exist on disk
merge-conflicts (Standard 11): no unresolved merge conflict markers in any of the 4 changed files

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex session 019e5b99 returned 1 MISSED_CRITICAL finding and 1 OBSERVATION_ONLY. The MISSED_CRITICAL claimed that settings.json:558 newly introduces a Standard 3 violation by registering python3 without venv activation for pretool-gitignore-preflight.py. The OBSERVATION_ONLY confirmed that all other draft assessments (settings.json:22 Chinese text pre-existing, userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh:39 python3 pre-existing, test_arch6_arch3.py:25 .py test file out of Standard 3 scope) are correct.

findings.issue: Codex claims settings.json:558 newly introduces a Standard 3 violation — the new hook command directly invokes python3 without venv activation
findings.proposed_fix: Add violation: standard: 'use-source-venv', severity: 'critical', location: 'settings.json:558', finding: 'New hook registration directly invokes python3 without venv activation', recommendation: 'Use a venv-backed command or formally document a Standard 3 exception for settings hook command strings'
findings.rationale: Standard 3 detection scope is explicitly 'Scan all .sh and .md files for python3 calls' — settings.json is a JSON configuration file, not a .sh or .md file, and is therefore outside Standard 3's defined scan scope. Additionally, the python3 invocation pattern in settings.json hook command strings is an established project-wide convention: 20+ pre-existing entries in the same file use the identical pattern (checked lines 316-554). The newly added entry at line 558 follows the same convention as all surrounding entries. Introducing a Standard 3 finding against a JSON config file would require rewriting the detection scope rule itself, which is a separate cycle concern. Codex's proposed fix would require inconsistent treatment of this one entry relative to all other hook command registrations.

findings.issue: Codex confirmed all other draft assessments are correct: settings.json:22 Chinese text is pre-existing, userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh:39 python3 is pre-existing, test_arch6_arch3.py:25 is out of Standard 3 scope
findings.rationale: Codex agreement with draft assessment on all other checked items.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Codex MISSED_CRITICAL finding rejected: Standard 3 scan scope is .sh and .md files only; settings.json JSON config is excluded from Standard 3 detection. The 20+ pre-existing python3 hook entries in settings.json confirm this is an established project convention. Zero violations remain after review.
